title: Knowledge Context CRUD

story.i_want_to: have a comprehensive Knowledge context module with CRUD operations, filtering, and audit logging
story.so_that: all knowledge wiki operations are centralized, tenant-scoped, and auditable

rationale: A well-structured context module is the backbone of any Phoenix domain. The Knowledge context centralizes all article and link operations behind a clean API, ensuring every mutation is tenant-scoped, wrapped in an Ecto.Multi transaction with audit logging, and consistently returns {:ok, result} or {:error, reason} tuples. The filtering capabilities (by project, category, status, tags) enable agents to efficiently search the knowledge base, while audit logging creates a complete history of knowledge evolution for compliance and debugging.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.Knowledge context module exists with public functions: create_article/3, get_article/2, list_articles/2, update_article/3, archive_article/2 (with optional opts), create_link/2, delete_link/2, list_links_for_article/2.

create_article(tenant_id, attrs, opts) creates an article in an Ecto.Multi transaction. Sets tenant_id programmatically (never from attrs). Logs creation in audit log via Audit.log_in_multi/3. Returns {:ok, article} or {:error, changeset}.
get_article(tenant_id, article_id) returns {:ok, article} with outgoing and incoming links preloaded, or {:error, :not_found}. Query is scoped by tenant_id.

list_articles(tenant_id, opts) returns a paginated list of articles. Supports filtering by: project_id (exact match, including nil for tenant-wide), category (exact match on enum value), status (exact match on enum value), tags (ANY overlap via PostgreSQL && operator). Supports limit/offset pagination with defaults (limit: 20, max: 100).

update_article(tenant_id, article_id, attrs, opts) updates an article in an Ecto.Multi transaction. Uses update_changeset for partial updates. Logs update in audit log. Returns {:ok, article} or {:error, changeset} or {:error, :not_found}.
archive_article(tenant_id, article_id, opts) sets the article status to :archived. Uses Ecto.Multi with audit logging. Returns {:ok, article} or {:error, :not_found}.

create_link(tenant_id, attrs) creates an article link in an Ecto.Multi transaction. Sets tenant_id programmatically. Validates both source and target articles exist and belong to the same tenant. Logs in audit log. Returns {:ok, link} or {:error, changeset}.
delete_link(tenant_id, link_id) deletes an article link. Scoped by tenant_id. Logs deletion in audit log via Ecto.Multi. Returns {:ok, link} or {:error, :not_found}.
list_links_for_article(tenant_id, article_id) returns all links where the article is either source or target, with the linked article preloaded. Scoped by tenant_id.
All queries use AdminRepo with explicit `where(tenant_id: ^tenant_id)` clause for tenant scoping.
All mutations are wrapped in Ecto.Multi transactions with Audit.log_in_multi/3 to ensure atomicity and auditability.
All context functions return {:ok, result} or {:error, reason} tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.

test_cases.name: Create article with valid attrs and verify audit log
steps: Call Knowledge.create_article(tenant.id, %{title: "Pattern: GenServer DI", body: "Use behaviours...", category: :pattern})
expected_results: Article has tenant_id set to tenant.id
Audit log entry exists for the creation event

test_cases.name: List articles with tag overlap filtering
expected_results: Returns article_a and article_b (both have 'elixir' tag)
Does NOT return article_c (no overlap with requested tags)

test_cases.name: List articles filtered by category and project_id
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, category: :pattern}) as article_a
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, category: :decision}) as article_b
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: nil, category: :pattern}) as article_c
expected_results: Returns only article_a (matches both project_id and category)
Does NOT return article_b (wrong category) or article_c (wrong project_id)

test_cases.name: Get article preloads outgoing and incoming links
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, source_article_id: article_a.id, target_article_id: article_b.id, relationship_type: :relates_to})
expected_results: Returns {:ok, article} with outgoing links containing the relates_to link to article_b
article_b also visible as incoming link when fetched via get_article

test_cases.name: Archive article sets status to archived
expected_results: Audit log entry exists for the archive event

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: tenant A cannot access tenant B articles
expected_results: list_articles returns empty list -- tenant_b cannot see tenant_a's articles

test_cases.name: Create link validates both articles belong to same tenant
steps: Call Knowledge.create_link(tenant_a.id, %{source_article_id: article_a.id, target_article_id: article_b.id, relationship_type: :relates_to})
expected_results: Returns {:error, _} -- target article not found in tenant_a's scope

test_cases.name: Pagination defaults and limits enforced
preconditions: 25 articles created for tenant via fixture
steps: Call Knowledge.list_articles(tenant.id, []) with no pagination opts
expected_results: limit: 200 call returns max 100 articles (max limit enforced)

technical_notes: Context module: `defmodule Loopctl.Knowledge do`. All functions take tenant_id as the first argument per multi-tenant rules.
Use AdminRepo with explicit `where(tenant_id: ^tenant_id)` clause. Do not rely solely on RLS for application-level tenant scoping.

All mutations use Ecto.Multi with Audit.log_in_multi/3 for transactional audit logging. Pattern: `Multi.new() |> Multi.insert(:article, changeset) |> Audit.log_in_multi(:article, "article.created") |> AdminRepo.transaction()`.
Tag filtering uses PostgreSQL array overlap operator: `where(fragment("? && ?", a.tags, ^tags))`. This leverages the GIN index from US-19.1.
Pagination: limit/offset with defaults (limit: 20, max: 100). Extract from opts with `min(Keyword.get(opts, :limit, 20), 100)` and `Keyword.get(opts, :offset, 0)`.
get_article preloads: `Repo.preload(article, [outgoing_links: :target_article, incoming_links: :source_article])`. Define the associations in the Article schema.
create_link must verify both articles exist in the tenant before inserting. Use a Multi step to fetch both articles, then insert the link.
All context functions MUST return {:ok, result} or {:error, reason} tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs in DataCase/ConnCase setup, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex, (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
